Farn Polrrrcal PRACrrcBs C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
September 28,2001
Ginny Vida, Executive Director
San Francisco Ethics Commission
30 Van Ness Ave., Ste. 3900
SanFrancisco, CA 94102
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-231
Dear Ms. Vida
Re:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r
QUESTTONS
1. You have submitted for approval a modified Form 460 for use by candidates
and committees in the City and County of San Francisco to comply with the campaign
reporting provisions of the Act.
2. You also have asked whether candidates and committees must include Form
460 Schedule B (Loans Received) or Schedule H (Loans Made) with their paper filings if
they have no information to disclose on those schedules.
CONCLUSIONS
1. The Form 460 is approved.
2. No. Filers that have no outstanding loans and have no loan activity to report
for the period covered by a report need not include Schedules B and H with the campaign
statement.
I Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Tifle 2, sections
I 8 I 0 9- I 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.


OurFileNo. A-01-231
Page 2
FACTS
The Form 460 (Recipient Committee Campaign Statement) was revised to
implement legislative changes that were signed into law last year (Chapter 853, Stats.
2000), as well as Propositron34, which was passed by the voters in the November 2000
general election.
The San Francisco Ethics Commission ("Ethics Commission") is the filing officer
for campaign disclosure statements filed by San Francisco City and County candidates
and committees. By ordinance, the Ethics Commission established an electronic
campaign filing program for city and county committees that meet certain financial
thresholds. The program also allows filers to print and file paper copies of campaign
statements that comply with the campaign disclosure requirements of the Act. Due to
time and budget constraints, the Ethics Commission will not be able to revise its print
software to enable its filers to print out the new version of the Form 460 by the
October 10,2001, filing deadline.
You have submitted a sample Form 460, which includes all of the information
required for local candidates and committees by the revised Form 460 approved by the
Fair Political Practices Commission on June 8, 2001. Filers will print the Form 460
currently generated by the Ethics Commission's electronic filing system; those that have
loan information to report will attach the new Form 460 Schedule B and/or H, as
appropriate, with their paper filings.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires candidates and committees to file periodic campaign statements
disclosing contributions received and expenditures made in connection with state and
local elections. ($ 84100 et seq.) A "campaign statement" is defined as "an itemized
report which is prepared on a form prescribed by the [Fair Political Practices]
Commission...." ($ 82006.) Campaign statements are filed on paper at specified times
and places depending on the location and activities of the particular candidate or
committee. ($S 84200-84215.)
On June 8,2001, the Commission approved revisions to the Form 460 (Recipient
Committee Campaign Statement) to implement legislative changes that were signed into
law during 2000 (Chapter 853, Stats. 2000), as well as Proposition34, which was passed
by the voters in the November 2000 general election. In order to fully comply with the
Act's campaign reporting provisions, candidates and committees must disclose the
information required by the revised Form 460 on their paper reports.
Because changes to the Ethics Commission's electronic Form 460 will not be
completed in time for the October 10, 2001, campaign filing deadline, you have requested
approval of a modified version the Form 460 for reports due on October 10. The sampie
form you have submitted will allow candidates and committees that file electronically


Our File No. A-01-231
Page 3
with the Ethics Commission to print the old Form 460 from the Ethics Commission's
electronic filing system, manually replacing Schedules B and H, where appropriate, with
schedules contained in the new Form 460.
The sample Form 460 complies with the reporting requirements applicable to city
and county candidates and committees and may be used for paper filings due on
October 10,2001.
You also have asked whether it is necessary for candidates and committees to
include Schedules B and H with their paper filings if they have no loan information to
disclose. Section 84211was amended to require detailed reporting of all outstanding
loans on each campaign statement {iled. If a candidate or committee had no outstanding
loans and did not receive or make new loans during the reporting period, Schedules B and
H need not be included with the paper filing. (See Form 460 Instructions for Summary
Page.)
If you have any questions concerning this letter, please call me at (916) 322-5660
ortoll-free at (866) 275-3772.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
0^1* Lt-)nnnl l ou)
By:
Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

1. You have submitted for approval a modified Form 460 for use by candidates
and committees in the City and County of San Francisco to comply with the campaign
reporting provisions of the Act.
2. You also have asked whether candidates and committees must include Form
460 Schedule B (Loans Received) or Schedule H (Loans Made) with their paper filings if
they have no information to disclose on those schedules.
sections.conclusion:
1. The Form 460 is approved.
2. No. Filers that have no outstanding loans and have no loan activity to report
for the period covered by a report need not include Schedules B and H with the campaign
statement.
OurFileNo. A-01-231

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. You have submitted for approval a modified Form 460 for use by candidates
and committees in the City and County of San Francisco to comply with the campaign
reporting provisions of the Act.
2. You also have asked whether candidates and committees must include Form
460 Schedule B (Loans Received) or Schedule H (Loans Made) with their paper filings if
they have no information to disclose on those schedules.

CONCLUSION: 1. The Form 460 is approved.
2. No. Filers that have no outstanding loans and have no loan activity to report
for the period covered by a report need not include Schedules B and H with the campaign
statement.
